patterns.decisionQuestion: Which row should be opened, assigned, marked reviewed, or escalated first based on owner, status, risk, SLA, evidence, and activity?
tableColumns: identity with avatar/logo and secondary metadata
one compact row action plus optional link/icon/menu
patterns.emptyStateCopy: No rows match these filters. Clear a filter or create a new exception from the toolbar.

typography: Admin H1 uses text-2xl or smaller; no text-display-l or routine text-3xl.
Table text defaults to text-sm; metadata/helper text uses text-xs.
density: At a desktop review viewport, first viewport shows heading, compact filters, table header, and multiple row identities/actions when data exists.
No summary/workload card may sit between filters and the main table.
spacing: Page root uses space-y-4 or space-y-5.
Filter-to-table rhythm is compact; avoid space-y-6/gap-6 on queue pages.
Do not stack search, two segmented ButtonGroups, and summary chips into three chrome rows above the table.
Do not wrap search plus segmented filters in an extra rounded white shell unless the shell contains meaningful grouped filter content.
borders: Use SurfaceCard/DataTable with outline-fg-grey-200 or border-fg-grey-200.
Avoid darker grey borders except empty, drop, or error states.
profileRegion: Use AppLayout topbar profile; do not rebuild custom topbar/profile controls.
tableFirstViewport: Rows cap identity to two text lines and one compact action cluster.
Use compact row actions and truncate metadata to preserve row density.
Long tables use one DataTable surface plus row dividers; do not render each row as a nested card.
Do not wrap DataTable in another large titled SurfaceCard when the DataTable header can own the title/subtitle.
Do not use table-fixed on rich operational queues to squeeze table and rail together.
rightRail: Insight rail uses responsive min/max or clamp constraints and stays subordinate to the table; it must not push the table below the fold.
At common desktop widths, the table must stay readable. Use a drawer/dialog, hide the rail until a wide viewport, or use a dedicated detail route.
Do not render selected-row insight as a normal card below the table at the 1366px review viewport.
rowActions: Row actions may show one action cluster only: one small primary command, or one compact link/icon/menu.
Do not render two text actions such as Preview and Open side by side in every row.
Do not stack Preview/Open/Review-style actions vertically in every row; secondary actions belong in the preview rail, detail page, or action route.
cellDensity: Risk cells choose one representation: compact score, label, or progress bar. Do not combine number, percent, and bar in the same table cell.
Secondary row metadata should be no more than two grey-token steps lighter than primary row text.
When status already uses warning/danger/success color, priority and risk-score labels should stay neutral except for the highest severity tier.
Primary row identity text usually uses text-sm font-medium; reserve font-semibold for a single dominant metric or action.

forbiddenShortcuts: Do not render an operational queue as plain name/status/date columns.
Do not place a large workload summary card between filters and the main DataTable.
Do not let the main queue table fall below the desktop review viewport.
Do not hide all row actions in a kebab menu.
Do not put /new, /create, /edit, /import, or /bulk routes in sidebar navigation.
Do not define custom SectionCard, Button, Badge, Table, or generic card shells.
Do not omit evidence, activity, or detail links from the insight rail.
Do not use text-display-l as the default queue title; use compact Forge CRM H1 scale.
Do not force a right insight rail beside the table at lg desktop widths.
Do not render selected-row insight as a below-table card on the list page.
Do not stack multiple row actions vertically in the action column.
Do not put both a text button and text link in the row action column.
Do not combine risk score, percentage, and progress bar in one DataTable cell.
Do not stack two large ButtonGroups above the queue table.
Do not wrap a compact filter toolbar in a redundant rounded-card shell.
Do not use four or more colored label families in the same queue row.
Do not use table-fixed on rich queue DataTables as a substitute for reducing columns or collapsing the rail.
Do not wrap a DataTable in a second large titled card shell when the DataTable already provides the table surface.
